title: EIP-8131: Add Auth Data to EIP-7623 Floor
description: Includes EIP-7702 authorization data in the EIP-7623 floor gas calculation, closing a loophole that allows ~9% larger blocks than intended. Each authorization tuple is charged a flat 4,040 gas toward the floor, derived from its 101-byte size. This prevents circumvention of floor pricing by combining calldata with authorization lists while leaving normal transaction patterns unaffected.
author: Toni Wahrstätter (@nerolation)
laymanDescription: Fixes a gap in Ethereum's gas pricing rules where certain transactions could make blocks about 9% bigger than intended. By counting authorization data toward the minimum gas fee, this change ensures all transaction data is fairly priced and blocks stay within their intended size limits.

endUsers.description: No noticeable impact for typical users. Transactions using EIP-7702 authorizations alongside large calldata may see slightly higher gas costs.

appDevs.description: No code changes required. Applications heavily combining calldata with EIP-7702 authorizations should verify gas estimates still hold.

walletDevs.description: Gas estimation logic must account for the new floor contribution from authorization tuples when constructing EIP-7702 transactions.

toolingInfra.description: Gas estimation tools and transaction simulators need to incorporate authorization data into floor gas calculations for accurate results.

layer2s.description: No direct impact. Layer 2 protocols posting calldata to L1 do not use EIP-7702 authorization lists, so floor pricing is unchanged for them.

stakersNodes.description: Blocks remain within intended size limits, preventing potential resource pressure from the ~9% oversized block loophole.

elClients.description: Must update floor gas calculation to include a flat per-authorization cost. Straightforward change to intrinsic gas and validity checks.

clClients.description: No consensus layer changes required. This is purely an execution layer gas accounting modification.

benefits: Closes a loophole allowing ~9% larger blocks than intended via authorization list abuse.
Preserves EIP-7623 block size guarantees without changing overall gas limits or security parameters.
Uses a simple flat cost per authorization, avoiding complex byte-level accounting overhead.
Normal transactions are unaffected since execution cost already dominates the floor for typical usage.

scaleL1.description: Enforces intended block size limits by closing a gas pricing loophole, ensuring L1 resource usage stays predictable and within bounds.
